title: The Desert Journey Continues
description: On pages 7–9, this part focuses on the desert journey continues, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: The Desert Journey Continues. Baroque Works’ senior agents are summoned while the Straw Hats and Vivi continue moving through the desert toward the heart of Alabasta’s crisis. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: For ages past,

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: Sandra River has nourished the lands of Alabama,

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: But in recent years,

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: River

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: Sewater

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: The river has lost its vigour and is now being driven inland by the ocean.

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: So then the mouth of the river where the Dugongs were...

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: Yes, it's actually seawater. You can't use it to integrate the lands around here.

dialogue.speaker: Baroque Works agent
dialogue.text: Is that why this town dried up?

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 2 Bon Clay
dialogue.text: ...No, not quite. This town has managed to get by,

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 2 Bon Clay
dialogue.text: Via storing the occasional rainwater.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 2 Bon Clay
dialogue.text: "Hey...

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 1
dialogue.text: Even just a few years ago, this place was still a lively town full of plant life.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 1
dialogue.text: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Yes, but then...

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Starting 3 years ago, our country has been suffering from a severe drought.

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: A drought so severe that no place in Alabasta has received a single drop of rain ever since...

dialogue.speaker: Miss Doublefinger
dialogue.text: But that port town we visited earlier seemed to be doing fine.

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: Nanchana´s a special case, as it receives its water from the neighbouring oasis town Katorea.

dialogue.speaker: Toto
dialogue.text: This country's been through droughts before but to receive not a single drop of rain was unheard of in the several thousand years of our history.

dialogue.speaker: Koza
dialogue.text: However, there was one place in Alabama that somehow still received rainfall, and in higher amounts than it would normally get.

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: That place is Alubarma,

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: The capital city of Alabama where the King resides.

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: For a while, the people thought of it as the King's miracle.

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: But that all changed after a certain incident...

dialogue.speaker: Baroque Works agent
dialogue.text: 2 years ago

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Port Town Nanohana

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Unload the supplies!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Crack!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Ahhh! Watch out!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: It's gonna fall over!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Get outta the way!

summary: This part develops the desert journey continues and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
